result:
Fleshed the brief at `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/2/patron-flesh/engine-refresh-bug.md`.

Key moves:
- **Named the reader** (me, watching a live engine) and made scroll stability load-bearing rather than cosmetic (#22).
- **Reframed** from "more targeted updates" to "stop the full re-render at the source" — explicitly rejecting scroll save/restore as a consumer-layer patch (#31).
- **Scoped hard** to this one panel; pushed websocket/SSE transport change and pause-refresh toggles out of scope (#23, #31).
- **Specified the behavior concretely**: append-only transcript DOM, in-place scalar diff, auto-tail-when-at-bottom (the chat-app pattern), fail-loud on repeated fetch errors (#2, #29).
- **No new chrome on the surface** — this is a bug fix on an existing surface (#26), and if any small "following / scrolled up" affordance falls out, it colocates with the transcript (#40).
- **Flagged assumptions** about the refresh mechanism (polling vs something else), transcript mutability, and the frontend framework — the planner should confirm the mechanism before coding but the target behavior is invariant.
